name: Combustion
description: A substance reacts with oxygen, typically releasing energy in the form of heat and light.

name: Combination
description: Two or more substances combine to form a single new substance.

name: Decomposition
description: A single compound breaks down into two or more simpler substances.

name: Displacement
description: An element trades places with another element in a compound.

name: Double Displacement
description: Two compounds exchange ions or elements to form completely new compounds.

name: Precipitation
description: Two soluble salts in aqueous solution combine to form an insoluble solid (precipitate).

name: Neutralization
description: An acid and a base react to produce salt and water.

name: Oxidation
description: An oxidation reaction is a chemical process where a reactant loses electrons, causing its oxidation state to increase.

name: Reduction
description: Reduction reactions are chemical processes where an atom, ion, or molecule gains electrons, resulting in a decrease in its oxidation state.

name: Redox
description: Involves the transfer of electrons between reactants.

name: Disproportionation
description: A specific type of redox process where a single chemical substance is simultaneously oxidized and reduced

name: Hydrolysis
description: A hydrolysis reaction is a chemical process where water is added to a substance to break its chemical bonds.

name: Complexation
description: A reaction where a central metal ion bonds with one or more surrounding molecules or ions (ligands) to form a complex ion.
